message:
perf(vdbe): FusedLiteralResultRow opcode + peephole pass (IMPL-13)

Opcode variant, dispatch handler, codegen peephole pass, and unit tests
for fusing `Integer(lit, reg) + ResultRow(reg, 1)` into a single fused
dispatch that skips the register write-read roundtrip for single-column
literal result rows.

Implementation:
- `Opcode::FusedLiteralResultRow = 196` + `name()` entry + COUNT bump.
- Register-span metadata: the fused op reads+writes register `p2`.
- Dispatch added to `try_execute_hot_opcode` in engine.rs, matching
  the existing ResultRow `take_reg_range` drain contract (single
  column, register cleared after emission).
- Peephole pass `ProgramBuilder::apply_fuse_literal_result_row()` on
  both `fsqlite-types::ProgramBuilder` and `fsqlite-vdbe::ProgramBuilder`.
  Rewrites in place (ResultRow -> Noop) so program-counter offsets
  and jump targets remain valid without rewiring.
- Conservative fusion guards: exact register match, `ResultRow.p2==1`,
  no P4 payload, P3==P5==0 on both ops, AND the ResultRow must not be
  a resolved jump target (a mid-pair jump would otherwise skip the
  Integer write).

Tests (5 new, all passing):
- opcode_fusion_literal_result_row_peephole_rewrites_pair
- opcode_fusion_literal_result_row_byte_equivalent_to_unfused
  (compiles unfused, runs, then compiles+peephole-fuses same program,
  byte-compares emitted rows)
- opcode_fusion_peephole_skips_multi_column_result_row
- opcode_fusion_peephole_skips_register_mismatch
- opcode_fusion_peephole_skips_jump_target_on_result_row

The peephole pass is NOT wired into `ProgramBuilder::finish()` in this
commit — codegen callers must opt in by calling the method explicitly.
That wiring (and deciding which execution paths benefit) is a follow-up.

Pre-existing unrelated test failure (`opcode_from_byte`) is not touched
by this change; it was already failing before (asserts `from_byte(192)
== None` but LikeConstFast=192 is decoded).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
